data_in.description: HMDB database file (hmdb_metabolites.zip or hmdb_compounds.p pickle)

data_out.description: Log or summary report confirming formula-filtering step completion

domain_knowledge: DatabaseFormulaSampler is a ViMMS class that loads and filters molecular formulas from the HMDB; it applies m/z range and other constraints to produce a set of candidate formulas for chemical sampling.
The HMDB (Human Metabolome Database) is a public repository of known metabolite structures and properties; downloading the full database dump is the starting point for formula extraction.
Unique formula count is the total number of distinct molecular formulae that survive all filtering steps; duplicates must be removed before counting to ensure reproducibility.
The m/z range filter (e.g., 100–1000) constrains which formulas are retained based on their computed mass-to-charge ratio; this is a standard pre-processing step in LC-MS metabolomics.

direct_checks: file_exists: verify that HMDB database file is accessible from glasgowcompbio/vimms-data repository (hmdb_metabolites.zip or equivalent raw HMDB download)
script_runs: verify that DatabaseFormulaSampler can be instantiated and executed on the downloaded HMDB file with parameters min_mz=100, max_mz=1000 without runtime errors
value_in_range: verify that the count of unique formulas returned by DatabaseFormulaSampler after filtering is exactly 73,822
file_format_is: verify that HMDB input file is in expected format (pickle, CSV, or ZIP archive as used by vimms.ChemicalSamplers.DatabaseFormulaSampler)
expert_review: Confirm that the filtering logic applied by DatabaseFormulaSampler (m/z range 100–1000 and any intensity or other thresholds) matches the documented formula-filtering step in the methods
Validate that the count of 73,822 unique formulas is reproducible across multiple runs (no randomness or database state dependency affecting the output)

inputs: HMDB database file (hmdb_metabolites.zip or hmdb_compounds.p pickle)
methodology_summary: Download the HMDB database file from the vimms-data public repository.
Load HMDB data into memory using ViMMS's built-in HMDB loading functions.
Instantiate DatabaseFormulaSampler with the loaded HMDB data and configure m/z range constraints (or other filter criteria as present in the benchmark).
Execute the sampler's filtering pipeline to extract and deduplicate molecular formulas.
Count unique formulas in the filtered output and record the result.
Validation: verify that the output formula count equals 73,822 as reported in the benchmark.

task_objective: Apply DatabaseFormulaSampler to HMDB data with m/z bounds 100–1000 and verify the formula count matches the reported value of 73,822.

workflow_description: 1. Download the HMDB database file (hmdb_metabolites.zip or equivalent pickle file) from the vimms-data repository. 2. Load the HMDB data using ViMMS's HMDB data-loading utilities. 3. Instantiate a DatabaseFormulaSampler with the loaded HMDB database and apply formula-filtering constraints (e.g., m/z range 100–1000 if specified). 4. Execute the sampler's internal filtering logic to produce the set of unique molecular formulas that pass all criteria. 5. Count the total number of unique formulas in the filtered result and confirm the count equals 73,822.
